name: Brass Nigerian Business Banking and Payments API
what_it_does: Brass Nigerian business banking and payments REST API for Nigerian SMEs, startups, and fintech developers to access business bank accounts, process Nigerian payments (bank transfers, PIX-equivalent Nigerian Instant Payment), manage payroll, and integrate Brass business banking services — enabling Nigerian business payment automation, account management, multi-user financial operations, and banking-as-a-service capabilities through Brass's SME-focused digital business bank. Enables AI agents to manage account for Nigerian business banking automation, handle bank transfer for Nigerian payment dispatch automation, access payroll for Nigerian employee salary automation, retrieve account statement for Nigerian business reconciliation automation, manage bulk payment for Nigerian mass payment automation, handle API banking for Nigerian BaaS integration automation, access webhook for payment event notification automation, retrieve balance for business account management automation, manage sub-account for Nigerian business unit accounting automation, and integrate Brass with Nigerian SME ERP, accounting, and financial management platforms for business banking automation.
use_cases: Managing Nigerian business bank accounts and payment operations for SME financial automation agents
Processing bulk salary payments to Nigerian employees via Brass business banking automation agents
Integrating Brass with Nigerian SME accounting software for real-time banking reconciliation automation agents
Building embedded Nigerian business banking features through Brass BaaS API for fintech product automation agents
not_for: Consumer personal banking (Brass is business banking for SMEs and startups)
Non-Nigerian markets (Brass is Nigeria-specific business banking platform)
High-volume enterprise corporate banking (Brass targets SMEs, not large corporates)
best_when: A Nigerian SME, startup, or fintech developer wanting AI agents to manage Nigerian business bank accounts, process bulk payments, automate payroll, and integrate Brass's SME-focused digital banking capabilities.
avoid_when: CBN BUSINESS BANKING REGULATIONS: Brass operates under CBN banking regulations via its microfinance bank license; automated non-regulated banking operation assumption creates regulatory violation; automated must comply with CBN e-banking and BVN requirements. BUSINESS ACCOUNT REQUIRED: Brass API requires active Brass business account with Nigerian CAC business registration; automated individual account assumption creates wrong account type; automated must have CAC-registered business with Brass account. NIGERIAN NAIRA ONLY: Brass processes NGN; automated USD operation assumption creates currency_not_supported; automated must handle NGN amounts. API IS LIMITED COMPARED TO FULL PRODUCT: Brass's programmatic API may not expose all product features available in dashboard; automated full-feature API assumption creates feature_not_available for some banking operations.

auth.notes: Brass uses API key + OAuth2 for business API authentication. REST API with JSON. Lagos, Nigeria HQ. Founded 2020 by Sola Akindolu and Emmanuel Okeke. Products: Business current account, payments (NIP, NEFT), bulk payroll, multi-user access, expense cards, team banking, BaaS. SDKs: None public. CBN regulated via microfinance bank license. Backed by investors including Tiger Global, Greycroft, Y Combinator, etc. Competes with Kuda for Business and Carbon for Nigerian business banking.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Basic business account; API access may require premium plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly business account fees; per-transfer fees; premium API access tiers
pricing.notes: Lagos NG. CBN via MFB license. SME-focused. Subscription + per-transfer. Nigerian business banking.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; payment and account APIs throttled

known_agent_gotchas: BUSINESS CAC REGISTRATION REQUIRED: Brass business account requires Nigerian Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) registration documents; automated individual signup assumption creates compliance_required; automated must have registered Nigerian business entity

NIP TRANSFER LIMITS: Nigerian Instant Payment (NIP) transfers have per-transaction limits (N10M+ for high-tier); automated unlimited transfer assumption creates NIP_limit_exceeded for large transactions; automated must implement NIP limit-aware payment splitting

BULK PAYMENT TIMING: Brass bulk payroll may have processing time rather than instant execution; automated immediate bulk payment assumption creates delayed salary crediting; automated must implement bulk payment status tracking

MULTI-USER APPROVAL WORKFLOW: Brass supports multi-user approval for payments; automated single-approver assumption misses payment approval workflow requirements; automated must handle pending_approval state for payments requiring secondary authorization

NGN KOBO OR NAIRA: Confirm whether Brass API uses kobo (subunit) or Naira for amounts; automated incorrect denomination assumption creates 100x errors; automated must verify amount unit from API documentation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Nigerian business banking. CBN, NFIU, NDPR. API key + OAuth2. Nigeria. Business account and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Nigerian business banking. CBN, NFIU. API key + OAuth2. Nigeria. Business account and payment data.
